name: Charminar
description: Charminar is the most iconic landmark of Hyderabad, built in 1591 by Muhammad Quli Qutb Shah to commemorate the founding of the city and to mark the end of a deadly plague. The monument features four grand arches facing four directions, four minarets rising 56 metres, and a mosque on the top floor. It stands at the heart of the old city surrounded by bustling bazaars selling bangles, pearls, and street food. The surrounding Laad Bazaar is famous for its bridal wear and lac bangles.
best_time: Early morning 8am-10am or evening 6pm-8pm to avoid crowds

name: Golconda Fort
description: Golconda Fort is a magnificent 16th century citadel and former capital of the Qutb Shahi kingdom, spanning over 11 kilometres of outer wall. The fort is famous for its acoustics — a hand clap at the entrance gate can be heard at the highest point 1 kilometre away. It features eight gates, multiple palaces, temples, mosques, and a sophisticated water supply system. The fort housed the legendary Koh-i-Noor and Hope diamonds before they were taken by Aurangzeb. The evening sound and light show narrates the fort's 400-year history.
best_time: Early morning 8am-11am to avoid heat; evening for sound and light show at 7pm

name: Hussain Sagar Lake
description: Hussain Sagar is a large artificial lake built in 1562 during the rule of Ibrahim Quli Qutb Shah, spanning 5.7 square kilometres. At its centre stands a 18-metre tall monolithic statue of Lord Buddha, one of the largest in the world, installed on a small island called Gibson Island. Boat rides from Lumbini Park and Necklace Road take visitors to the Buddha statue. The lake promenade called Necklace Road is popular for evening walks, food stalls, and amusement rides. The lake divides the twin cities of Hyderabad and Secunderabad.
best_time: Evening 5pm-8pm for sunset views and boat rides

name: Qutb Shahi Tombs
description: The Qutb Shahi Tombs are a necropolis containing the tombs of the seven rulers of the Qutb Shahi dynasty, located 800 metres north of Golconda Fort. The complex spans over 90 acres and features some of the finest examples of Indo-Islamic architecture with Persian, Pashtun, and Hindu influences. Each tomb is set in a garden with a mosque and a bathhouse. The tombs were recently restored by the Aga Khan Trust for Culture and the grounds are immaculately maintained. Best visited in combination with Golconda Fort on the same day.

name: Chowmahalla Palace
description: Chowmahalla Palace was the official residence of the Nizams of Hyderabad and the seat of the Asaf Jahi dynasty. The palace complex covers 14 acres and consists of two courtyards — the southern and northern — each containing magnificent palaces, durbar halls, clocktowers, and fountains. The Khilwat or Durbar Hall is particularly impressive with its 19 Italian chandeliers and Belgian glass. The palace houses a museum with vintage cars, royal carriages, antique furniture, and the wardrobe and weapons of the Nizams. It was declared a heritage site by the Government of India.

name: Salar Jung Museum
description: Salar Jung Museum is one of the three National Museums of India and houses one of the largest one-man collections of antiques in the world, assembled by Mir Yousuf Ali Khan Salar Jung III. The collection spans 35,000 art objects, 9,000 manuscripts, and 47,000 printed books from across the world including sculptures, paintings, jade, ivory, glassware, clocks, textiles, and arms and armour. The famous Veiled Rebecca marble sculpture and the double-statue of Mephistopheles and Margaretta are prized exhibits. The museum also has a musical clock that strikes the hour with a mechanical figure.

name: Birla Mandir
description: Birla Mandir is a stunning white marble Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Venkateswara, built by the Birla Foundation and completed in 1976. The temple is constructed entirely from 2000 tonnes of Rajasthani white marble and sits atop Naubath Pahad hill, offering panoramic views of Hussain Sagar Lake and the city skyline. The temple architecture blends South Indian, Rajasthani, and Utkala styles. The interiors feature intricate carvings of Hindu deities and scriptures. The surrounding gardens are beautifully maintained and the hilltop location makes it one of the best photography spots in the city.

name: Ramoji Film City
description: Ramoji Film City is the world's largest integrated film studio complex, certified by the Guinness World Records, spanning over 1666 acres. It is a fully functional studio with over 47 permanent outdoor sets representing different cities and countries, used for filming hundreds of Indian films and television serials. Visitors can take guided tours of active film sets, watch live action stunt shows, visit theme parks, gardens, and amusement rides. The complex has hotels, restaurants, and entertainment zones. It is located 30 kilometres east of Hyderabad city centre and requires a full day visit.
best_time: Morning 9am opening to make the most of the full day

name: Mecca Masjid
description: Mecca Masjid is one of the oldest and largest mosques in India, built between 1617 and 1694 during the reign of the Qutb Shahi and Mughal rulers. The mosque can accommodate up to 10,000 worshippers at a time. It is named after the bricks used in its central arch, which were made from soil brought from Mecca. The mosque is built from a single monolithic granite slab weighing 750 tonnes for its foundation. The Nizam's tombs are housed in an enclosure to the left of the mosque. The mosque is located adjacent to Charminar and is best visited alongside it.

name: Nehru Zoological Park
description: Nehru Zoological Park is one of the largest zoos in India, spread over 380 acres of natural forest. It houses over 1500 animals belonging to 169 species including tigers, lions, leopards, rhinoceroses, hippopotamuses, giraffes, and a wide variety of birds and reptiles. The zoo is known for its successful captive breeding programs for endangered species. Visitors can take a toy train ride through the zoo, visit the prehistoric animal park, butterfly park, and nocturnal animal house. The natural setting with lakes and forest makes it a pleasant outing for families.
